title: Views
text: Views Some DBMSs support views, which are named tables that are derived from one or more underlying base tables by a query. They do not exist as a normal base table. Uniface does not recognize that a view has been derived from one or more base tables. Uniface only recognizes database tables that have been defined in the application model; therefore, if you want to present a view to Uniface, the DBMS connector must present it as a normal table. It is up to the underlying DBMS to deal with any management of a view in the database itself. Connector Interface Requirements The connector interface does not require the connector to create views in any situation. The Uniface user may, however, create views in the database, and use them with Uniface. In this case, the designer defines the modeled entity so that it describes the view and not the base table. The designer probably defines the entity as non-updatable. Uniface does not use physical row addresses (PRAs) with non-updatable entities. If possible, the connector must be capable of handling such views. In situations where this book refers to operations on a table, the connector should be prepared to handle a view.
